headline: Sarawak River Pollution Index Worsens 5th Straight Year
context: DOE data shows 23 of Sarawak 45 monitored rivers recorded deteriorating water quality for the fifth consecutive year in 2025.

cards.big: 23 of 45 Sarawak rivers recorded worsening pollution for 5 straight years
cards.sub: Agricultural runoff and industrial discharge are primary contamination sources

cards.big: Rajang River BOD levels exceeded safe limits at 8 of 12 monitoring stations
cards.sub: Malaysia longest river serves 650,000 people as their primary water source

cards.big: Palm oil mill effluent violations numbered 340 in 2025 up from 220 in 2023
cards.sub: Average fine of RM15,000 per violation provides little deterrent to producers

cards.big: Water treatment costs in Sibu rose 45% to RM38 million due to contamination
cards.sub: Increased chemical treatment raises health concerns about long-term safety

cards.big: River pollution tests whether enforcement can match the pace of contamination
Five consecutive years of worsening river pollution — enforcement without penalties produces only reports.
